title: Hadoop MapReduce Job Submission
description: This directory manages job submission, ensuring correct ownership and permissions. It includes JobSubmitter.java for job submission, QueueInfo.java and QueueState.java for queue management, and SharedCacheConfig.java for parsing shared cache configurations. Task-related components like TaskCompletionEvent.java, TaskCounter.java, and TaskID.java track task completion and status. The 'counters' directory uses Abstract Factory and Factory patterns for managing counters. The 'filecache' directory, with ClientDistributedCacheManager and DistributedCache, distributes large, read-only files. ClientProtocolProvider.java adheres to the Factory pattern, while CountersStrings.java in the 'util' directory provides utility methods for counter string representation. This architecture ensures scalability, maintainability, and reliability, leveraging Java and Hadoop technologies. It employs coordinator, worker, and utility patterns, with the coordinator role for job submission and management, and the utility role for providing counter string representation.

TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Job Submission

DESCRIPTION: This directory manages job submission, ensuring correct ownership and permissions. It includes JobSubmitter.java for job submission, QueueInfo.java and QueueState.java for queue management, and SharedCacheConfig.java for parsing shared cache configurations. Task-related components like TaskCompletionEvent.java, TaskCounter.java, and TaskID.java track task completion and status. The 'counters' directory uses Abstract Factory and Factory patterns for managing counters. The 'filecache' directory, with ClientDistributedCacheManager and DistributedCache, distributes large, read-only files. ClientProtocolProvider.java adheres to the Factory pattern, while CountersStrings.java in the 'util' directory provides utility methods for counter string representation. This architecture ensures scalability, maintainability, and reliability, leveraging Java and Hadoop technologies. It employs coordinator, worker, and utility patterns, with the coordinator role for job submission and management, and the utility role for providing counter string representation.
